title: 12+ LinkedIn Follow-Up Message Templates That Get Replies (2026)
description: Copy-paste LinkedIn follow-up message templates for after connection acceptance. Tested sequences for booking meetings, sharing value, and staying top of mind.

intro.title: Why Follow-Up Messages Are Where Deals Happen
intro.content:
The connection request gets you in the door. The follow-up message closes the deal. Most LinkedIn outreach fails not because the connection request was bad, but because the follow-up was generic, pushy, or non-existent.

Here's the reality: 80% of sales require 5+ follow-ups, but 44% of salespeople give up after just one attempt. On LinkedIn, the numbers are even starker — most people send a connection request, maybe one message, and then move on. That's leaving money on the table.

The templates below cover every stage of the follow-up sequence: the first message after connection, the value-add follow-up, the meeting request, and the gentle breakup. Each one is designed to feel personal, provide value, and move the conversation forward.

templates.name: The Warm Thank You (First Message After Connection)
templates.text:
Thanks for connecting, {{firstName}}! I've been following {{company}}'s growth in {{industry}} — impressive trajectory.

Quick question: how are you currently handling {{painPoint}}? We've been helping teams like yours with {{solution}} and I'd love to share what's working.

No pressure at all — just happy to connect either way.
templates.whenToUse: Send within 24 hours of connection acceptance. This is your first real conversation — set the tone right.
templates.personalizationTips: Reference something specific about their company. The {{painPoint}} should be a real challenge you know their industry faces.

templates.name: The Value-First Follow-Up
templates.whenToUse: 2-3 days after your first message if no reply. Leads with value instead of a sales pitch.
templates.personalizationTips: The content must be genuinely relevant to their role and industry. Don't share your company blog post unless it actually addresses their pain point.

templates.name: The Case Study Drop
templates.text:
Hi {{firstName}}, wanted to share a quick win — we recently helped {{similarCompany}} in {{industry}} achieve {{specificResult}} using {{product/approach}}.

Their situation was similar to what I've seen at {{company}}. Would you be open to a 15-min call to see if we could replicate that for your team?
templates.whenToUse: When you have a relevant case study. Works best as a 2nd or 3rd follow-up.
templates.personalizationTips: The case study should be from their industry or a similar company size. Specific results (numbers, percentages) outperform vague claims.

templates.name: The Soft Meeting Request
templates.text:
Hey {{firstName}}, I know you're busy — so I'll keep this short.

Would you be open to a quick 15-minute chat this week? I think there's a real opportunity to help {{company}} with {{specificChallenge}}, and I'd rather show you than tell you.

If this week doesn't work, happy to find a time that does. No rush.
templates.whenToUse: After you've sent 1-2 value-add messages. This is the ask.
templates.personalizationTips: Keep it under pressure-free. '15 minutes' is the magic number — it feels low-commitment. Never say '30 minutes' in a cold follow-up.

templates.name: The Insight Trigger
templates.text:
Hi {{firstName}}, came across something interesting — {{relevantInsight}}.

Made me think of {{company}} and how this might impact your {{department/focus}}. Have you been thinking about this?

Would love to hear your take.
templates.whenToUse: When you find a relevant industry trend, news item, or data point that connects to the prospect's work.
templates.personalizationTips: The insight should be genuinely interesting and not obviously a sales setup. Think: industry reports, regulatory changes, competitor moves.

templates.name: The Mutual Connection Lever
templates.text:
Hey {{firstName}}, I was chatting with {{mutualConnection}} the other day and your name came up — they mentioned you're doing great work with {{initiative}} at {{company}}.

I work with similar teams on {{valueArea}} and would love to swap notes. Any interest in a quick chat?
templates.whenToUse: When you can naturally reference a mutual connection who's relevant to both of you.
templates.personalizationTips: Only use if the mutual connection reference is authentic. Prospects will verify with the person mentioned.

templates.name: The Problem-Agitate Follow-Up
templates.text:
Hi {{firstName}}, one thing I keep hearing from {{jobTitle}}s in {{industry}} is that {{commonProblem}}.

It usually gets worse when {{agitation}} — and by then, the team is stuck playing catch-up.

We built {{product}} specifically to solve this. Worth a conversation?
templates.whenToUse: When your prospect hasn't responded to softer approaches. This template creates urgency through problem awareness.
templates.personalizationTips: The problem must be real and specific to their role. Generic problems ('generating more leads') won't land — be specific ('managing outreach across 8 LinkedIn accounts without a unified inbox').

templates.name: The Social Proof Nudge
templates.text:
Hey {{firstName}}, just in case it helps — here's what {{customerName}}, {{customerTitle}} at {{customerCompany}}, said about working with us:

"{{testimonialQuote}}"

Happy to share more details on how we helped them. Would a quick call make sense?
templates.whenToUse: When you have a strong testimonial from someone in a similar role or industry.
templates.personalizationTips: Use a testimonial from someone at a comparable company. If possible, choose someone the prospect might recognize or respect.

templates.name: The Quick Video Message
templates.whenToUse: When you want to stand out from text-based messages. Video messages have 3x higher open rates on LinkedIn.
templates.personalizationTips: Record a genuinely personalized video — mention their name, company, and a specific observation. Generic 'personalized' videos with just a name overlay don't work as well.

templates.name: The Gentle Breakup
templates.text:
Hi {{firstName}}, I've reached out a few times and I know you're busy — so I don't want to keep cluttering your inbox.

If {{solution}} isn't a priority right now, totally understand. But if things change down the road, I'm here.

Wishing you and the team at {{company}} all the best.
templates.whenToUse: After 3-4 unanswered messages. This is your last message in the sequence — and surprisingly, it often gets the most replies.
templates.expectedPerformance: 12-20% reply rate (often the highest in a sequence)
templates.personalizationTips: The breakup message works because it removes pressure. Keep it genuine and respectful. Some prospects reply weeks later.

templates.name: The Re-Engagement (After Silence)
templates.text:
Hey {{firstName}}, it's been a while since we connected. Hope things are going well at {{company}}!

We've recently launched {{newFeature/update}} that I thought might be relevant for your {{department}}. Worth revisiting?

Happy to share what's new over a quick call.
templates.whenToUse: When re-engaging a connection who went cold 1-3 months ago. Gives a fresh reason to reconnect.
templates.personalizationTips: Reference something that's actually new — a product update, a new case study, or a company milestone. Don't just repeat your original pitch.

templates.name: The Congratulations Opener
templates.text:
{{firstName}}, congrats on {{achievement}} — that's a big milestone for {{company}}! 🎉

With that kind of growth, I imagine {{relatedChallenge}} is top of mind. We've been helping similar teams tackle that — happy to share what we're seeing.

Either way, well deserved!
templates.whenToUse: When the prospect or their company has a recent achievement (funding, award, promotion, product launch).
templates.personalizationTips: The congratulations must be genuine and timely. Link the achievement to a natural conversation about how you can help.

customization.title: How to Customize These Templates
customization.content:
Follow-up messages have more room than connection requests (no character limit), but shorter is still better. Here's how to customize effectively:

1. **First message after connection**: Keep to 3-5 sentences. Your goal is to start a conversation, not pitch.
2. **Value-add follow-ups**: Share content, insights, or case studies that are genuinely relevant. Self-serving content kills reply rates.
3. **Meeting requests**: Make it easy to say yes. Suggest a specific time, keep it to 15 minutes, and remove all friction.
4. **Timing between follow-ups**: Wait 2-4 days between messages in the same sequence. Don't send daily.
5. **Sequence length**: 4-5 messages is optimal. Less than 3 and you give up too early. More than 6 and you're annoying.

Always include an easy out ('no pressure,' 'totally understand if not') — it paradoxically increases response rates.

sendingAtScale.title: Sending Follow-Up Sequences with Handshake
sendingAtScale.content:
Handshake automates your entire follow-up sequence:

- **Multi-step sequences**: Set up 4-5 follow-up messages with customizable delays between each step
- **Conditional logic**: If they reply, the sequence stops automatically. No awkward double-messages.
- **Dynamic personalization**: Use `{{firstName}}`, `{{company}}`, `{{jobTitle}}`, and custom fields across all messages
- **A/B test follow-ups**: Test different message variants at each step to optimize reply rates
- **Unified inbox**: All replies from all senders land in one inbox — respond instantly without switching accounts
- **Multi-sender rotation**: Follow-up messages are sent from the same sender who made the original connection, maintaining conversational continuity

faqs.question: How long should I wait between LinkedIn follow-up messages?
faqs.answer: 2-4 days between messages in the same sequence is the sweet spot. Less than 2 days feels pushy. More than 7 days and you lose momentum. For re-engagement after a cold period, wait 2-4 weeks.

faqs.question: How many follow-up messages should I send before giving up?
faqs.answer: 4-5 messages is the optimal sequence length. Include a mix of value-add content, a case study, a meeting request, and a breakup message. 80% of replies come after the 2nd message or later.

faqs.question: What's a good reply rate for LinkedIn follow-up messages?
faqs.answer: 10-20% overall reply rate across your sequence is a solid benchmark for cold outreach. The first message typically gets 15-25%, declining with each follow-up. The breakup message often sees a bump to 12-20%.

faqs.question: Should follow-up messages come from the same sender as the connection request?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sending a connection request from one account and a follow-up from another is confusing and damages trust. Handshake's multi-sender rotation maintains sender continuity throughout the entire sequence.

faqs.question: What's the best time to send LinkedIn follow-up messages?
faqs.answer: Tuesday through Thursday, 8-11 AM in the prospect's timezone, typically gets the best engagement. Monday mornings are crowded; Friday afternoons are ignored. Avoid weekends unless your audience is active then.
